_meta.purpose: Phase B per-tissue calibration table for the q-eAON field model. One parameter set per target tissue, so cross-organ mixtures are combined across independently-calibrated fields (Phase A partitions; Phase B calibrates). NOT yet wired into the runtime; values with status 'skeleton' are placeholders to be derived. Provenance carries the anchoring dataset + DOI for every calibrated value.
_meta.field_model: d(phi)/dt = m*mu0*(1-phi) + s*phi*(1-phi) - rho*phi ; hazard h = c*phi*m*gate ; risk = 1-exp(-int h dt). m=1+(RMU-1)*gen ; s=S0+s_prom*prom ; gate=1+b_est*prom.
_meta.calibration_principle: mu0,S0 <- normal-tissue somatic-mutation/clonal data (rate x stem-cell target size x driver fraction; dN/dS + clone sizes). c <- fit to tissue background age-specific incidence. s_prom,b_est,RHO0 <- tissue exposure-response/cessation cohort where one exists, else transfer lung ratios (flagged). RMU treated as tissue-invariant to first order.

pan_tissue_mutation_atlases.ref: Li et al. 2021, A body map of somatic mutagenesis in morphologically normal human tissues, Nature 597:398
SMaHT Network 2025, Nature 643 (3 Jul 2025) — 19 tissues / 150 donors reference catalogue; NEW, monitor for per-tissue variant data releases

incidence_databases.ref: SEER (NCI) — SEER*Explorer, Nov-2024 submission (released Apr-2025), age-specific rates through 2022
GLOBOCAN 2022 / Bray et al. 2024, CA Cancer J Clin 74:229; IARC Cancer Today portal

mu0_S0_RHO0.source: Yoshida et al. 2020, Nature 578:266 (bronchial driver fractions, near-normal recovery)

single_cell_support.source: Huang/Yoshida et al. 2022, Nat Genet 54:492 (single-cell bronchial mutations, ageing+smoking)

c_s_prom_b_est.source: Peto et al. 2000, BMJ 321:323 (never-/continuing-/ex-smoker lung-cancer risk)

validation_target.desc: asbestos x smoking and radon x smoking supra-additive lung-cancer interaction

tissues.status: calibrated (background field); promotion knobs transferred from lung (flagged)
tissues.note: phi reinterpreted as clonal-haematopoiesis (CHIP) fraction; CHIP->AML is a documented two-stage-like progression. HSC target pool ~50,000-200,000 (Lee-Six 2018) anchors mu0 target size.

mu0.source: SEER AML median age at diagnosis 70

c.basis: fit so lifetime AML risk = 0.5% to age 85

calibration.transfer_flag: promotion knobs REMAIN lung-transferred: benzene (g=1, genotoxic-dominant) does not constrain them. Constrain later from a pure promoter (liver aflatoxin x HBV).
calibration.benzene_exposure_response_finding: Confronting the calibrated blood field with the Hayes 1997 / meta-analysis cohort (RR 1.64/1.90/2.62 for <40/40-100/>100 ppm-yr; 7.1 at >=25 ppm) shows the CURRENT LINEAR genotoxic channel (gen=x) OVERSHOOTS ~300x (predicts RR ~600-770 = near-certain leukemia at occupational dose). A SATURATING genotoxic response gen=Gmax*x/(x+x50) (~Gmax 2.4, x50 O(1e3)) reproduces the observed dose-response within ~1.5x across 25x of dose and tames ambient over-prediction (RR 1.37->1.04 at 3 ug/m3). This is a GLOBAL model-structure decision (affects high-dose behaviour of all chemicals/tissues; lung@x~1 and ambient minimally affected). Complementary to kappa-pin (kappa=vertical scale at anchor; saturation=shape away from anchor). See qeaon-recalibration/CALIB_HAEM_BENZENE_NOTE.md.
calibration.validation_age_shape: cumulative cases vs SEER 18/22% @55, 37/38% @65, 65/64% @75 (85+ gap = competing mortality; not fit to shape)
calibration.phi_note: phi ~72% @70, ~92% @85 -- higher than exome-VAF CHIP prevalence but consistent with near-universal clonal expansion by ultra-sensitive WGS (Mitchell 2022); phi magnitude sensitive to S0 (S0=0.05 -> phi(85)~40%)

normal_tissue_mutation.role: HSC population size + mutation rate (mu0, S0)
clonal dynamics across lifespan + selection (S0, age-slope)

exposure_response.source: Hayes et al. 1997, JNCI 89:1065 (benzene dose-related haematologic neoplasms, Chinese cohort); Linet et al. 2015, Int J Cancer 137:2184

validation_target.desc: benzene nonlinear low-dose hematotoxicity (steeper slope at low dose)

calibration.regime: promotion-limited (HCC): low effective initiation, expansion-sensitive; clean-room uses a measured mutation multiplier m for genotoxins, not RMU

mu0.basis: implied HCC-driver fraction ~3e-7 of the 33 SNV/yr hepatocyte mutation load (passenger-dominated)

g_normal.basis: fit to sporadic HCC median onset ~68; quiescent liver, << blood 0.10
g_normal.source: Brunner 2019 (limited normal clonal expansion); SEER HCC median age ~66

c.basis: fit to sporadic (non-chronic-disease) normal-liver HCC lifetime ~0.3%

calibration.validation: median onset 68 (SEER ~66); ~52% of lifetime risk by age 65; population-mix ~8% chronic-disease livers at 15% HCC reproduce SEER total ~1.5%; risk ceiling 99% (promotion-sensitive)
calibration.interaction_prediction: aflatoxin x HBV multiplier 29x (HBV-carrier HCC 10%) .. 58x (25%), g_HBV pinned from HBV-carrier HCC epidemiology (no aflatoxin data) -> brackets JECFA 30x and combined ~60x. See liver_bg2.py / liver_predict.py / LIVER_INTERACTION_NOTE.md.

normal_tissue_mutation.role: mu0, S0 (healthy + cirrhotic liver mutation/clonal dynamics)

exposure_response.source: Aflatoxin x HBV, Qidong cohort (Ross/Qian/Groopman); review Kew 2003, Liver Int 23:405

validation_target.desc: aflatoxin B1 x HBV multiplicative synergy for HCC (AFB1-alone ~4x, HBV-alone ~5x, combined ~60x) — a direct test of the eAON initiator x promoter interaction magnitude

normal_tissue_mutation.source: Li et al. 2021 body map / SMaHT 2025

tissues.note: upper-aerodigestive; oesophageal normal-tissue data used as the best available proxy for selection dynamics.

TCDD, PCB-126 are non-genotoxic promoters with no single target tissue. OPEN DECISION: apply as a promotion modifier across the other tissue fields, or hold as one aggregate field. Do not calibrate as a standalone tissue until this is decided.
